steps.title: Identify the starting amount
steps.content: At the beginning of the observation, when $t=0$, there are $84{,}000$ bacteria. So the model needs an initial factor of $84{,}000$.

steps.title: Use the daily multiplier
steps.content:
The population doubles every day, so each day multiplies the previous amount by $2$. After $t$ days, that multiplier is
$$
2^t
$$
Therefore the model is
$$
y=84{,}000(2)^t
$$

steps.title: Choose the exponential growth model
steps.content: The expression uses the initial amount and raises the daily doubling factor to the number of days. The answer is D.
